exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com am, is ou are:
exercises.sentence: I _____ a student.
exercises.explanation_pt: Com 'I' sempre usamos 'am'. I am = Eu sou/estou.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com am, is ou are:
exercises.sentence: She _____ from Brazil.
exercises.explanation_pt: Com he/she/it usamos 'is'. She is = Ela é.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com am, is ou are:
exercises.sentence: They _____ my friends.
exercises.explanation_pt: Com they/we/you usamos 'are'. They are = Eles são.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com am, is ou are:
exercises.sentence: We _____ happy today.
exercises.explanation_pt: Com 'we' usamos 'are'. We are happy = Nós estamos felizes.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com am, is ou are:
exercises.sentence: The cat _____ on the table.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'The cat' = it, então usamos 'is'. The cat is = O gato está.

exercises.question: Qual frase está gramaticalmente correta?
exercises.explanation_pt: Com 'I' sempre usamos 'am'. 'I am a doctor' = Eu sou médico.

exercises.question: She _____ happy. (negativa)
exercises.explanation_pt: Negativa do TO BE: sujeito + is/am/are + not. She is not (isn't) happy.

exercises.question: Como perguntar 'Você é estudante?'
exercises.explanation_pt: Pergunta com TO BE: invertemos. Are you...? Is he...? Am I...?

exercises.sentence: My parents _____ teachers.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'My parents' = they, então usamos 'are'.

exercises.sentence: It _____ very cold today.
exercises.explanation_pt: Com 'it' usamos 'is'. It is cold = Está frio.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com a forma correta do verbo:
exercises.sentence: She _____ to school every day. (go)
exercises.explanation_pt: No presente simples, com he/she/it, adicionamos -s ou -es ao verbo. She goes.

exercises.sentence: They _____ coffee every morning. (drink)
exercises.explanation_pt: Com they/we/I/you, o verbo fica na forma base (sem -s). They drink.

exercises.sentence: He _____ English at university. (study)
exercises.explanation_pt: Verbos terminados em -y precedido de consoante: troca -y por -ies. He studies.

exercises.question: I _____ like spicy food.
exercises.explanation_pt: Negativa no presente simples com I/you/we/they: don't + verbo base. I don't like.

exercises.question: She _____ eat meat.
exercises.explanation_pt: Negativa com he/she/it: doesn't + verbo base (sem -s). She doesn't eat.

exercises.question: Como perguntar 'Você gosta de música?'
exercises.explanation_pt: Pergunta no presente simples com you: Do you + verbo base? Do you like...?

exercises.sentence: _____ your brother work here? (pergunta)
exercises.explanation_pt: Pergunta com he/she/it: Does + sujeito + verbo base? Does your brother work...?

exercises.sentence: Maria _____ three languages. (speak)
exercises.explanation_pt: Maria = she → speaks (com -s). Maria speaks three languages.

exercises.question: Escolha a frase correta:
exercises.explanation_pt: Com doesn't, o verbo volta para a forma base (sem -s). He doesn't like.

exercises.sentence: The store _____ at 9 PM. (close)
exercises.explanation_pt: The store = it → closes (com -s). The store closes at 9 PM.

exercises.sentence: I _____ English right now. (study)
exercises.explanation_pt: Present Continuous: am/is/are + verbo-ing. I am studying = Eu estou estudando (agora).

exercises.sentence: She _____ a book at the moment. (read)
exercises.explanation_pt: She + is + reading. She is reading = Ela está lendo (neste momento).

exercises.sentence: They _____ TV right now. (watch)
exercises.explanation_pt: They + are + watching. They are watching = Eles estão assistindo.

exercises.question: She _____ to school every day. (rotina)
exercises.explanation_pt: Para ROTINAS usamos Present Simple. She goes every day. Present Continuous é para AGORA.

exercises.question: Look! It _____! (agora)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Look!' indica que está acontecendo AGORA → Present Continuous. It is raining!

exercises.sentence: We _____ dinner right now. (have)
exercises.explanation_pt: We + are + having. 'Right now' indica ação em progresso → Present Continuous.

exercises.question: He _____ working today.
exercises.explanation_pt: Negativa do Present Continuous: is not (isn't). He isn't working today.

exercises.question: _____ you studying for the exam?
exercises.explanation_pt: Pergunta no Present Continuous: Are you + verbo-ing? Are you studying?

exercises.sentence: The children _____ in the park. (play)
exercises.explanation_pt: The children + are + playing. Ação em progresso no momento.

exercises.question: 'I eat breakfast at 7 AM' vs 'I am eating breakfast now'. Qual é a diferença?
options: B) O primeiro é futuro, o segundo é passado
C) O primeiro é formal, o segundo é informal
D) O primeiro é rotina, o segundo é agora
exercises.explanation_pt: Present Simple = rotina/hábito. Present Continuous = ação acontecendo agora. Essa é a diferença fundamental.

exercises.sentence: I _____ to the supermarket yesterday. (go)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Go' é irregular no passado: go → went. I went = Eu fui.

exercises.sentence: She _____ for the exam last night. (study)
exercises.explanation_pt: Verbos regulares: adicionar -ed. Study → studied. She studied = Ela estudou.

exercises.sentence: We _____ a great movie last weekend. (see)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'See' é irregular: see → saw. We saw = Nós vimos.

exercises.sentence: They _____ arrive on time. (not)
exercises.explanation_pt: Negativa no Past Simple: didn't + verbo base. They didn't arrive = Eles não chegaram.

exercises.question: _____ you go to the party last night?
exercises.explanation_pt: Pergunta no Past Simple: Did + sujeito + verbo base? Did you go?

exercises.sentence: He _____ his homework before dinner. (do)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Do' é irregular: do → did. He did his homework = Ele fez a lição.

exercises.sentence: The class _____ at 8 AM this morning. (start)
exercises.explanation_pt: Verbo regular: start → started. The class started = A aula começou.

exercises.question: Escolha a frase correta no passado:
options: A) She not went to school.
B) She didn't go to school.
C) She didn't went to school.
D) She don't go to school.
exercises.explanation_pt: Com 'didn't', o verbo volta para a forma BASE (não o passado). She didn't go (não 'went').

exercises.sentence: I _____ born in São Paulo. (be)
exercises.explanation_pt: Passado de 'am' = 'was'. I was born = Eu nasci.

exercises.sentence: The students _____ very tired after the exam. (be)
exercises.explanation_pt: Passado de 'are' = 'were'. The students were tired = Os alunos estavam cansados.

exercises.sentence: I think it _____ rain tomorrow. (previsão)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Will' para previsões/opiniões sobre o futuro. I think it will rain.

exercises.sentence: She _____ to study medicine next year. (plano definido)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Going to' para planos/intenções já decididos. She is going to study medicine.

exercises.question: Don't worry, I _____ help you. (decisão no momento)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Will' para decisões tomadas no momento da fala. 'I'll help you' = decisão instantânea.

exercises.sentence: We _____ visit our grandparents this weekend. (plano)
exercises.explanation_pt: Plano já decidido → 'are going to'. We are going to visit = Nós vamos visitar.

exercises.question: Look at those clouds! It _____ rain. (evidência visual)
exercises.explanation_pt: Quando há EVIDÊNCIA visual do que vai acontecer → 'going to'. As nuvens indicam chuva.

exercises.sentence: I _____ be at the meeting tomorrow. (negativa)
exercises.explanation_pt: Negativa de 'will' = won't (will not). I won't be = Eu não estarei.

exercises.question: _____ you come to the party tonight?
exercises.explanation_pt: Pergunta com 'will': Will + sujeito + verbo base? Will you come?

exercises.sentence: The exam _____ be difficult, I'm sure. (certeza)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Will' para previsões com certeza pessoal. The exam will be difficult.

exercises.sentence: They _____ move to a new apartment next month. (plano)
exercises.explanation_pt: Plano decidido para o futuro → 'are going to'. They are going to move.

exercises.question: Qual é a diferença principal entre 'will' e 'going to'?
options: B) Will = decisões instantâneas e previsões; Going to = planos e evidências
C) Will é para hoje, going to é para amanhã
D) Will é formal, going to é informal
exercises.explanation_pt: Will: decisões no momento + previsões/opiniões. Going to: planos já decididos + previsões baseadas em evidência. Essa é a regra principal.

exercises.sentence: I _____ to Paris twice. (be)
exercises.explanation_pt: Present Perfect: have/has + past participle. I have been = Eu já fui/estive. Usado para experiências de vida.

exercises.sentence: She _____ this movie before. (see)
exercises.explanation_pt: She + has + seen (particípio de see). She has seen = Ela já viu.

exercises.sentence: They _____ here since 2020. (live)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Since 2020' indica ação que começou no passado e continua → Present Perfect. They have lived.

exercises.question: I _____ my keys. I can't find them! (resultado presente)
exercises.explanation_pt: Present Perfect para ação passada com RESULTADO no presente. Perdi as chaves e AGORA não as encontro.

exercises.question: I _____ to London in 2019. (data específica)
exercises.explanation_pt: Com DATA ESPECÍFICA (in 2019) usamos Past Simple, não Present Perfect. I went in 2019.

exercises.sentence: _____ you ever _____ sushi? (eat)
exercises.explanation_pt: Pergunta sobre experiência de vida: Have you ever + particípio? Have you ever eaten sushi?

exercises.sentence: He _____ finished his homework yet. (negativa)
exercises.explanation_pt: He + hasn't (has not) + particípio. 'Yet' (ainda) combina com Present Perfect negativo.

exercises.question: Qual frase está correta?
options: A) I have going to Japan.
B) I have went to Japan.
C) I have gone to Japan.
D) I have go to Japan.
exercises.explanation_pt: Have + PAST PARTICIPLE. Go → gone (particípio). I have gone = Eu fui (e ainda estou lá ou já voltei).

exercises.sentence: We _____ known each other for ten years. (know)
exercises.explanation_pt: We + have + known. 'For ten years' indica duração → Present Perfect. Nos conhecemos há 10 anos.

exercises.question: Quando usamos o Present Perfect?
options: B) Para experiências de vida, ações recentes com resultado presente, e ações que continuam
D) Para ações com data específica
exercises.explanation_pt: Present Perfect: 1) Experiências (I have been to Paris), 2) Resultado presente (I have lost my keys), 3) Ações que continuam (I have lived here since 2020).

exercises.sentence: São Paulo is _____ than Curitiba. (big)
exercises.explanation_pt: Adjetivos curtos (1 sílaba): +er. Big → bigger. São Paulo is bigger than Curitiba.

exercises.sentence: English is _____ than Chinese for Brazilians. (easy)
exercises.explanation_pt: Adjetivos terminados em -y: troca -y por -ier. Easy → easier.

exercises.sentence: This book is _____ than that one. (interesting)
exercises.explanation_pt: Adjetivos longos (3+ sílabas): more + adjetivo. More interesting than.

exercises.sentence: She is the _____ student in the class. (good)
exercises.explanation_pt: Good é irregular: good → better → best. She is the best = Ela é a melhor.

exercises.sentence: It was the _____ movie I have ever seen. (bad)
exercises.explanation_pt: Bad é irregular: bad → worse → worst. The worst = O pior.

exercises.question: Qual comparação está correta?
options: A) He is more tall than me.
B) He is more taller than me.
C) He is taller than me.
D) He is tallest than me.
exercises.explanation_pt: Tall = 1 sílaba → taller than. NUNCA 'more taller' (dobro de comparativo).

exercises.sentence: This is the _____ expensive restaurant in the city. (superlativo)
exercises.explanation_pt: Superlativo de adjetivos longos: the most + adjetivo. The most expensive = o mais caro.

exercises.sentence: My car is _____ fast _____ yours. (tão... quanto)
exercises.explanation_pt: Igualdade: as + adjetivo + as. As fast as = tão rápido quanto.

exercises.question: Qual superlativo está correto?
options: A) She is the more intelligent person I know.
B) She is the more intelligentest person I know.
C) She is the intelligentest person I know.
D) She is the most intelligent person I know.
exercises.explanation_pt: Intelligent = adjetivo longo → the most intelligent. The most intelligent person I know.

exercises.sentence: Today is _____ than yesterday. It's freezing! (cold)
exercises.explanation_pt: Cold = 1 sílaba → colder than. Today is colder than yesterday.

exercises.question: If it rains tomorrow, I _____ at home.
exercises.explanation_pt: First Conditional (situação real/possível): If + present simple, will + verbo base. If it rains, I will stay.

exercises.question: If I _____ more money, I would travel the world.
exercises.explanation_pt: Second Conditional (situação hipotética): If + past simple, would + verbo base. If I had = Se eu tivesse.

exercises.sentence: If she studies hard, she _____ the exam. (pass — real possibility)
exercises.explanation_pt: First Conditional (possibilidade real): If + present, will + verbo. She will pass.

exercises.sentence: If I _____ you, I would accept the job offer. (be — hypothetical)
exercises.explanation_pt: Second Conditional: If I were (não 'was' — forma especial). If I were you = Se eu fosse você.

exercises.question: 'If I win the lottery, I'll buy a house.' Isso é:
options: B) First Conditional — possível mas improvável
exercises.explanation_pt: First Conditional porque usa 'if + present, will'. É possível (alguém pode ganhar na loteria), embora improvável.

exercises.sentence: If you heat water to 100°C, it _____. (boil — fato científico)
exercises.explanation_pt: Zero Conditional (fatos/verdades): If + present, present. If you heat water, it boils. Sempre verdade.

exercises.question: If I _____ speak English fluently, I would apply for the scholarship.
exercises.explanation_pt: Second Conditional: If + past simple. 'Can' no passado = 'could'. If I could speak = Se eu pudesse falar.

exercises.sentence: I _____ go to the beach if the weather is nice. (real plan)
exercises.explanation_pt: First Conditional (plano real): will + verbo. I will go if the weather is nice.

exercises.question: Qual frase está correta?
options: A) If I see him, I will tell him.
B) If I will see him, I tell him.
C) If I saw him, I will tell him.
D) If I would see him, I will tell him.
exercises.explanation_pt: First Conditional: If + PRESENT (não 'will'), will + verbo. NUNCA 'If I will...'.

exercises.question: Qual é a diferença entre First e Second Conditional?
options: B) First é para situações possíveis/reais, Second é para situações hipotéticas/imaginárias
C) First é formal, Second é informal
D) First é para o passado, Second é para o futuro
exercises.explanation_pt: First Conditional: situações reais/possíveis (If it rains...). Second Conditional: situações imaginárias/hipotéticas (If I were rich...).

exercises.question: 'They built this hospital in 1950.' Na voz passiva:
options: A) This hospital was built in 1950.
B) This hospital is built in 1950.
C) This hospital has built in 1950.
exercises.explanation_pt: Passiva no Past Simple: was/were + past participle. This hospital was built = Este hospital foi construído.

exercises.sentence: English _____ in many countries. (speak — presente)
exercises.explanation_pt: Passiva no presente: is/are + past participle. English is spoken = Inglês é falado.

exercises.sentence: The book _____ by millions of people. (read — present)
exercises.explanation_pt: The book is read = O livro é lido. Passiva: is + past participle.

exercises.sentence: The Mona Lisa _____ by Leonardo da Vinci. (paint — past)
exercises.explanation_pt: Passiva no passado: was + past participle. The Mona Lisa was painted = foi pintada.

exercises.question: 'The exam was taken by 200 students.' Isso é:
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Was taken' = was + particípio → voz passiva. O sujeito (exam) recebe a ação.

exercises.sentence: The new hospital _____ next year. (build — futuro passivo)
exercises.explanation_pt: Passiva no futuro: will be + past participle. Will be built = será construído.

exercises.question: 'Someone stole my wallet.' Na passiva:
exercises.explanation_pt: Steal → stole → stolen (particípio). My wallet was stolen = Minha carteira foi roubada.

exercises.sentence: These cars _____ in Japan. (make — presente)
exercises.explanation_pt: Cars = plural → are + made. These cars are made in Japan = Estes carros são feitos no Japão.

exercises.question: Por que usamos a voz passiva?
options: D) Quando o agente da ação não é importante ou é desconhecido
exercises.explanation_pt: Usamos passiva quando: 1) O agente não importa ('English is spoken worldwide'), 2) O agente é desconhecido ('My car was stolen'), 3) Em textos acadêmicos/formais.

exercises.sentence: The results _____ tomorrow. (announce — futuro)
exercises.explanation_pt: The results will be announced = Os resultados serão anunciados. Futuro passivo: will be + particípio.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com who, which, that ou where:
exercises.sentence: The doctor _____ treated me was very kind.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Who' para pessoas. The doctor who treated me = O médico que me tratou.

exercises.sentence: The book _____ I read last week was excellent.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Which' (ou 'that') para coisas. The book which I read = O livro que eu li.

exercises.sentence: This is the hospital _____ my mother works.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Where' para lugares. The hospital where my mother works = O hospital onde minha mãe trabalha.

exercises.sentence: The student _____ father is a doctor wants to study medicine.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Whose' para posse. The student whose father = O aluno cujo pai. Indica que algo pertence a alguém.

exercises.question: The city _____ I was born is very small.
exercises.explanation_pt: Cidade = lugar → 'where'. The city where I was born = A cidade onde eu nasci.

exercises.question: She said: 'I am tired.' → She said that she _____ tired.
exercises.explanation_pt: Reported Speech: o tempo verbal 'volta' um passo. 'Am' → 'was'. She said she was tired.

exercises.question: He said: 'I will call you tomorrow.' → He said he _____ call me the next day.
exercises.explanation_pt: Reported Speech: 'will' → 'would'. 'Tomorrow' → 'the next day'. He said he would call.

exercises.question: 'I have finished the exam,' she said. → She said she _____ finished the exam.
exercises.explanation_pt: Reported Speech: 'have' → 'had'. Present Perfect → Past Perfect. She said she had finished.

exercises.instruction_pt: Transforme a pergunta para Reported Speech:
exercises.question: 'Where do you live?' he asked. → He asked me where I _____.
exercises.explanation_pt: Reported questions: sem inversão, tempo verbal volta. 'Do you live' → 'I lived'. He asked where I lived.

exercises.question: No Reported Speech, o que acontece com os tempos verbais?
options: C) Voltam um passo para o passado
exercises.explanation_pt: Backshift: present → past, past → past perfect, will → would, can → could. Os tempos 'voltam' um passo.

exercises.question: If I _____ harder, I would have passed the exam.
exercises.explanation_pt: Third Conditional (situação irreal no PASSADO): If + past perfect, would have + particípio. If I had studied = Se eu tivesse estudado.

exercises.question: If she _____ the email, she would have come to the meeting.
exercises.explanation_pt: Third Conditional: If + had + particípio. If she had read = Se ela tivesse lido.

exercises.question: If they had left earlier, they _____ the flight.
exercises.explanation_pt: Consequência do Third Conditional: would have + particípio. They wouldn't have missed = Eles não teriam perdido.

exercises.sentence: If I had known about the problem, I _____ you. (tell)
exercises.explanation_pt: Third Conditional: ...would have + particípio. I would have told you = Eu teria te contado.

exercises.question: 'I didn't bring an umbrella. Now I'm wet.' Reescreva com conditional:
options: A) If I had brought an umbrella, I wouldn't be wet now.
B) If I had brought an umbrella, I wouldn't have been wet.
C) If I bring an umbrella, I won't be wet.
D) If I brought an umbrella, I wouldn't be wet.
exercises.explanation_pt: Mixed Conditional: If + past perfect (passado irreal), would + infinitivo (resultado presente). Ação passada com resultado agora.

exercises.sentence: We _____ the project on time if we had started earlier. (finish)
exercises.explanation_pt: Would have finished = Teríamos terminado. Third Conditional para situação passada irreal.

exercises.question: 'If I had been born in the US, I would speak English fluently.' Isso é:
exercises.explanation_pt: Third Conditional: If + had been born (past perfect), would speak (resultado). Situação irreal no passado.

exercises.sentence: She _____ the job if she had had more experience. (get)
exercises.explanation_pt: Would have gotten = Teria conseguido. 'If she had had' = Se ela tivesse tido (past perfect de 'have').

exercises.question: Qual Third Conditional está correto?
options: A) If I had known, I would told you.
B) If I had known, I would have told you.
C) If I knew, I would have told you.
D) If I would have known, I told you.
exercises.explanation_pt: Estrutura correta: If + had + particípio, would have + particípio. NUNCA 'If I would have...'

exercises.question: Combine: 1st = possível, 2nd = hipotético presente, 3rd = ?
exercises.explanation_pt: 1st: situação possível. 2nd: hipotético no presente. 3rd: hipotético no PASSADO — algo que NÃO aconteceu e não pode ser mudado.

theme: Modal Verbs (must, should, might, can, could)

exercises.question: You _____ see a doctor. That cough sounds serious. (conselho forte)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Should' para conselho. You should see a doctor = Você deveria ir ao médico.

exercises.question: Students _____ wear uniforms at this school. It's a rule. (obrigação)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Must' para obrigação/regra. Students must wear uniforms = Alunos devem usar uniforme (é obrigatório).

exercises.question: She _____ be at home. I saw her car in the driveway. (dedução forte)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Must' para dedução forte (quase certeza). O carro está lá → ela DEVE estar em casa.

exercises.question: It _____ rain later. The forecast isn't sure. (possibilidade)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Might' para possibilidade (não certeza). It might rain = Pode ser que chova (talvez).

exercises.sentence: You _____ not park here. It's a no-parking zone. (proibição)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Must not' para proibição. You must not park = Você não pode estacionar (é proibido).

exercises.question: 'You must study' vs 'You should study'. Qual é mais forte?
exercises.explanation_pt: Must = obrigação forte. Should = conselho/recomendação. 'You must' é mais forte que 'You should'.

exercises.sentence: _____ you help me carry these boxes? (pedido educado)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Could' para pedidos educados. Could you help me? = Você poderia me ajudar? (mais educado que 'Can you...?')

exercises.question: She _____ have studied more. Her grade was very low. (conselho sobre o passado)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Should have + particípio' = deveria ter feito (mas não fez). She should have studied = Ela deveria ter estudado mais.

exercises.question: He _____ have taken the bus. I'm not sure how he got here. (possibilidade passada)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Might have + particípio' = talvez tenha feito. He might have taken = Ele pode ter pegado (incerteza sobre o passado).

exercises.question: Ordene do mais fraco ao mais forte: might, should, must
exercises.explanation_pt: Do mais fraco ao mais forte: might (talvez) → should (deveria) → must (deve/tem que). Might = 30% certeza, Should = 70%, Must = 95%.

theme: Gerunds and Infinitives (enjoy doing vs want to do)

exercises.sentence: I enjoy _____ books before bed. (read)
exercises.explanation_pt: Depois de 'enjoy' sempre usamos GERÚNDIO (-ing). I enjoy reading = Eu gosto de ler.

exercises.sentence: She wants _____ a doctor in the future. (become)
exercises.explanation_pt: Depois de 'want' sempre usamos INFINITIVO (to + verbo). She wants to become = Ela quer se tornar.

exercises.sentence: He avoids _____ in public. (speak)
exercises.explanation_pt: Depois de 'avoid' → gerúndio. He avoids speaking = Ele evita falar.

exercises.sentence: They decided _____ to a new city. (move)
exercises.explanation_pt: Depois de 'decide' → infinitivo. They decided to move = Eles decidiram se mudar.

exercises.question: I stopped _____. (fumar — parei de fumar)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Stop + gerúndio' = parar de fazer algo. I stopped smoking = Parei de fumar. (Stop + infinitivo = parar PARA fazer algo diferente.)

exercises.sentence: Would you mind _____ the window? It's hot. (open)
exercises.explanation_pt: Depois de 'mind' → gerúndio. Would you mind opening? = Você se importaria de abrir?

exercises.sentence: I can't afford _____ a new car right now. (buy)
exercises.explanation_pt: Depois de 'afford' → infinitivo. I can't afford to buy = Não posso me dar ao luxo de comprar.

exercises.question: 'I remember locking the door' vs 'I remember to lock the door'. Qual é a diferença?
options: A) Gerúndio é formal, infinitivo é informal
C) Gerúndio é britânico, infinitivo é americano
D) Gerúndio = lembro que tranquei (passado). Infinitivo = lembro de trancar (futuro).
exercises.explanation_pt: Remember + gerúndio = lembrar de algo que JÁ FEZ. Remember + infinitivo = lembrar de algo que PRECISA fazer. Grande diferença!

exercises.sentence: She suggested _____ to a different restaurant. (go)
exercises.explanation_pt: Depois de 'suggest' → gerúndio. She suggested going = Ela sugeriu ir.

exercises.question: Quais verbos usam GERÚNDIO? Escolha o grupo correto:
options: C) enjoy, avoid, mind, suggest, finish
exercises.explanation_pt: Gerúndio (-ing): enjoy, avoid, mind, suggest, finish, consider, imagine, practice, deny, risk. Infinitivo (to): want, need, decide, hope, plan, agree, refuse, promise, expect.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com a, an, the ou Ø (nada):
exercises.sentence: I saw _____ interesting documentary last night.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'An' antes de vogal sonora. 'Interesting' começa com som de 'i' → an interesting.

exercises.sentence: _____ sun rises in the east.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'The' para coisas únicas (só existe um sol). The sun, the moon, the Earth.

exercises.sentence: She is studying _____ medicine at university.
exercises.explanation_pt: Sem artigo antes de matérias acadêmicas. She is studying Ø medicine (não 'the medicine').

exercises.sentence: _____ Amazon is the largest rainforest in the world.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'The' com rios, oceanos, florestas, desertos, cadeias de montanhas. The Amazon, The Pacific, The Sahara.

exercises.sentence: I need to buy _____ milk and _____ eggs.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Some' com substantivos incontáveis (milk) e contáveis plurais (eggs) em frases afirmativas.

exercises.question: Qual frase está correta?
options: A) I love an music. (em geral)
B) I love the music. (em geral)
C) I love a music. (em geral)
exercises.explanation_pt: Sem artigo para falar de coisas em GERAL. 'I love music' (música em geral). 'I love the music at this party' (música específica).

exercises.sentence: There isn't _____ sugar left. Can you buy some?
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Any' em frases negativas e perguntas com incontáveis/plurais. There isn't any sugar = Não tem nenhum açúcar.

exercises.sentence: _____ people think that climate change is not real.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'A few' com contáveis plurais. 'A little' com incontáveis. A few people = Algumas pessoas.

exercises.question: 'Few students passed' vs 'A few students passed'. Diferença?
options: A) 'Few' é formal, 'A few' é informal
C) 'Few' é singular, 'A few' é plural
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Few' = quase nenhum (tom negativo). 'A few' = alguns (tom positivo). Few students passed = quase ninguém passou. A few students passed = alguns passaram.

exercises.sentence: _____ of the students in my class speak English fluently.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Most' (sem 'the') = a maioria de um grupo geral. 'Most of the students' ou 'Most students' = a maioria dos alunos.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com o phrasal verb correto:
exercises.sentence: Scientists are trying to _____ why some people are immune to the virus. (descobrir)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Find out' = descobrir, averiguar. Scientists are trying to find out = Cientistas estão tentando descobrir.

exercises.sentence: The professor asked us to _____ our assignments by Friday. (entregar)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Hand in' = entregar (trabalho, tarefa). Hand in your assignments = Entreguem suas tarefas.

exercises.sentence: We need to _____ this problem before it gets worse. (resolver)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Figure out' = resolver, entender, descobrir a solução. Figure out the problem = Resolver o problema.

exercises.sentence: The meeting was _____ until next week due to the holiday. (adiada)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Put off' = adiar. The meeting was put off = A reunião foi adiada.

exercises.sentence: She had to _____ the job offer because of family reasons. (recusar)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Turn down' = recusar (oferta, convite). She turned down the offer = Ela recusou a oferta.

exercises.sentence: The company plans to _____ a new product next month. (lançar)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Bring out' = lançar (produto). Bring out a new product = Lançar um novo produto.

exercises.sentence: Don't _____ — keep trying until you succeed! (desistir)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Give up' = desistir. Don't give up = Não desista!

exercises.sentence: The experiment _____ interesting results. (produzir/resultar em)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Come up with' = produzir, inventar, chegar a (resultado/ideia). Came up with results = Produziu resultados.

exercises.sentence: We need to _____ the main causes of the problem. (identificar)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Point out' = identificar, apontar, destacar. Point out the causes = Identificar as causas.

exercises.sentence: The lecture _____ three main topics. (abordou/tratou de)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Deal with' = lidar com, abordar, tratar de. The lecture dealt with = A aula abordou/tratou de.

exercises.question: I wish I _____ more languages. (saber — desejo presente)
exercises.explanation_pt: Wish + past simple para desejos sobre o PRESENTE. I wish I knew = Eu gostaria de saber (mas não sei).

exercises.question: She wishes she _____ harder for the exam. (estudar — arrependimento)
exercises.explanation_pt: Wish + past perfect para arrependimentos sobre o PASSADO. She wishes she had studied = Ela gostaria de ter estudado (mas não estudou).

exercises.sentence: If only I _____ taller! (be — desejo presente)
exercises.explanation_pt: If only + were (para todos os sujeitos, incluindo I). If only I were taller = Se ao menos eu fosse mais alto.

exercises.question: I wish it _____ stop raining. (desejo sobre situação irritante)
exercises.explanation_pt: Wish + would para expressar irritação com algo que você quer que mude. I wish it would stop = Queria que parasse.

exercises.sentence: He wishes he _____ accepted the job offer. (arrependimento)
exercises.explanation_pt: Wish + had + particípio para arrependimentos. He wishes he had accepted = Ele gostaria de ter aceitado.

exercises.question: 'I wish I spoke English' vs 'I wish I had spoken English at the meeting'. Qual fala do passado?
exercises.explanation_pt: Wish + past simple = desejo presente. Wish + past perfect = arrependimento passado. 'Had spoken' é passado.

exercises.sentence: I wish I _____ afford to study abroad. (can — desejo presente)
exercises.explanation_pt: Wish + could para desejos sobre habilidade presente. I wish I could = Eu gostaria de poder.

exercises.question: Qual expressa arrependimento sobre o passado?
options: A) I wish I had had more time.
B) I wish I will have more time.
C) I wish I had more time.
D) I wish I have more time.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'I wish I had had more time' = Eu gostaria de ter tido mais tempo (passado). 'I wish I had more time' = presente.

exercises.sentence: If only we _____ about the problem earlier. (know — passado)
exercises.explanation_pt: If only + past perfect para arrependimentos. If only we had known = Se ao menos soubéssemos antes.

exercises.question: Complete a regra: Wish + past simple = _____. Wish + past perfect = _____.
exercises.explanation_pt: Wish + past simple = desejo/lamento sobre o PRESENTE. Wish + past perfect = arrependimento sobre o PASSADO. Wish + would = irritação/pedido de mudança.

exercises.sentence: The experiment was successful. _____, the results need to be replicated. (contraste)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'However' = no entanto/porém. Indica contraste. O experimento foi bem-sucedido, PORÉM os resultados precisam ser replicados.

exercises.sentence: The drug reduced symptoms by 40%. _____, it improved sleep quality. (adição)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Furthermore' = além disso. Adiciona informação. O remédio reduziu sintomas E ALÉM DISSO melhorou o sono.

exercises.sentence: The population is aging. _____, healthcare costs are increasing. (consequência)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Consequently' = consequentemente. A população envelhecendo → consequentemente custos de saúde aumentam.

exercises.sentence: _____ the evidence is limited, the results are promising. (concessão)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Although' = embora/apesar de. Although the evidence is limited = Embora a evidência seja limitada.

exercises.sentence: The study had a small sample size. _____, its findings should be interpreted with caution. (conclusão)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Therefore' = portanto. Amostra pequena → PORTANTO resultados devem ser interpretados com cautela.

exercises.question: Escolha todos que indicam contraste:
options: B) For example, Such as, In particular
C) Therefore, Consequently, As a result
D) However, Nevertheless, On the other hand
exercises.explanation_pt: Contraste: However, Nevertheless, On the other hand, Although, Despite. Adição: Furthermore, Moreover. Consequência: Therefore, Consequently.

exercises.sentence: _____ the high cost, many students choose to study abroad. (apesar de)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Despite' + substantivo/gerúndio. Despite the high cost = Apesar do alto custo. (Compare: 'Although' + sujeito + verbo.)

exercises.sentence: Renewable energy is clean and sustainable. _____, it can reduce dependence on fossil fuels. (adição)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Moreover' = além disso/ademais (mais formal que 'also'). Energia renovável é limpa e ADEMAIS reduz dependência.

exercises.sentence: The theory was widely accepted for decades. _____, recent evidence has called it into question. (contraste)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Nevertheless' = no entanto/mesmo assim. A teoria era aceita, NO ENTANTO evidências recentes a questionaram.

exercises.question: Categorize: Therefore, However, Furthermore, Although, Consequently, Moreover, Despite, Nevertheless
options: D) Adição: Furthermore/Moreover | Contraste: However/Nevertheless/Although/Despite | Consequência: Therefore/Consequently
exercises.explanation_pt: Adição: Furthermore, Moreover, In addition. Contraste: However, Nevertheless, Although, Despite, On the other hand. Consequência: Therefore, Consequently, As a result, Thus.

exercises.instruction_pt: Transforme a pergunta em embedded question:
exercises.question: 'Where does she live?' → I don't know _____.
exercises.explanation_pt: Embedded question: SEM inversão. I don't know where she lives (não 'where does she live'). A ordem volta ao normal: sujeito + verbo.

exercises.question: 'What time does the class start?' → Could you tell me _____?
options: A) what time is the class starting
B) what time the class starts
C) what time start the class
D) what time does the class start
exercises.explanation_pt: Could you tell me what time the class starts? Embedded question = sem inversão, sem 'do/does'.

exercises.sentence: I wonder _____ he passed the exam. (se — whether/if)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'I wonder whether/if' = Eu me pergunto se. Whether é mais formal que if para perguntas indiretas.

exercises.question: Nobody knows _____ the meeting was canceled.
exercises.explanation_pt: Nobody knows why the meeting was canceled. Embedded question com 'why' — ordem normal depois.

exercises.sentence: It is important _____ students understand the material. (que)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'It is important that...' = É importante que. 'That' introduz noun clause como sujeito ou complemento.

exercises.question: 'How much does it cost?' → Do you know _____?
options: D) how much does it cost
exercises.explanation_pt: Do you know how much it costs? Sem inversão, sem 'does'. Verbo recebe -s porque 'it' é terceira pessoa.

exercises.sentence: The fact _____ she speaks three languages is impressive.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'The fact that...' = O fato de que. 'That' conecta o fato com a descrição.

exercises.question: Qual embedded question está correta?
options: A) I don't know is the library where.
B) I don't know where is the library.
C) I don't know where the library is.
D) I don't know where the library.
exercises.explanation_pt: Ordem correta em embedded question: question word + sujeito + verbo. Where the library IS (não 'where is the library').

exercises.sentence: _____ the professor said surprised everyone.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'What the professor said' = O que o professor disse. 'What' como sujeito da frase (noun clause como sujeito).

exercises.question: Qual é a regra principal das embedded questions?
options: A) Usar inversão como em perguntas diretas
C) Usar ordem normal (sujeito + verbo) sem do/does/did
D) Usar ponto de interrogação no final
exercises.explanation_pt: Regra: embedded questions usam ORDEM NORMAL (sujeito + verbo), sem auxiliar do/does/did, e sem ponto de interrogação. 'I wonder where she lives' (não 'where does she live').

exercises.question: Not only _____ the exam, but she also got the highest score.
exercises.explanation_pt: Inversão com 'Not only': Not only + auxiliar + sujeito + verbo. Not only did she pass = Ela não só passou.

exercises.question: Rarely _____ such an impressive presentation.
exercises.explanation_pt: Inversão com advérbios negativos: Rarely + auxiliar + sujeito. Rarely have I seen = Raramente vi.

exercises.sentence: Never before _____ such a devastating earthquake. (the city / experience)
exercises.explanation_pt: Never before + had + sujeito + particípio. Inversão com 'never before'.

exercises.question: _____ was Einstein who developed the theory of relativity.
exercises.explanation_pt: Cleft sentence: 'It was... who/that...' para dar ênfase. It was Einstein who = Foi Einstein quem.

exercises.question: _____ I need is more time to study. (o que)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'What I need is...' = O que eu preciso é. 'What' cleft sentence para enfatizar o objeto.

exercises.sentence: Only after finishing the experiment _____ the error. (the researchers / discover)
exercises.explanation_pt: Inversão com 'Only after': Only after... + auxiliar + sujeito + verbo. Did the researchers discover.

exercises.question: 'The economic crisis caused the unemployment.' Com ênfase no que causou:
options: A) It was the economic crisis that caused the unemployment.
B) The unemployment was caused by it
C) The economic crisis it was that caused.
D) What caused was the economic crisis
exercises.explanation_pt: Cleft sentence: It was + [elemento enfatizado] + that + resto. It was the economic crisis that caused = Foi a crise econômica que causou.

exercises.sentence: Under no circumstances _____ cheat on an exam. (students / should)
exercises.explanation_pt: Under no circumstances + inversão. Should students = devem os alunos. Em nenhuma circunstância devem os alunos colar.

exercises.question: 'It was in 1969 that humans first landed on the Moon.' O que está enfatizado?
exercises.explanation_pt: Cleft sentence enfatiza o que vem depois de 'It was': neste caso, 'in 1969' = o ano está enfatizado.

exercises.question: Em quais situações usamos inversão em inglês?
options: A) Depois de advérbios negativos (never, rarely, not only, hardly) e em frases enfáticas
B) Em todas as frases formais
exercises.explanation_pt: Inversão ocorre: 1) Após advérbios negativos (never, rarely, seldom, not only, hardly, scarcely). 2) Após 'only' (only after, only when). 3) Em cleft sentences (It was... that). Comum em escrita acadêmica/TOEFL.

theme: Revisão geral — Erros comuns de brasileiros no TOEFL

exercises.question: Escolha a frase correta:
options: A) I need informations more about the program.
B) I need more information about the program.
C) I need more informations about the program.
D) I need more an information about the program.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Information' é INCONTÁVEL em inglês — nunca tem 's'. I need more information (não 'informations'). Outros: advice, research, knowledge, furniture, equipment.

exercises.question: Escolha a frase correta:
options: A) She did a fault on the exam.
B) She made a error on the exam.
C) She did a mistake on the exam.
D) She made a mistake on the exam.
exercises.explanation_pt: MAKE a mistake (não 'do a mistake'). Make: make a decision, make money, make a mistake, make progress. Do: do homework, do research, do an exam, do the dishes.

exercises.question: Escolha:
options: A) He said me he was tired.
B) He told me that he was tired.
C) He said me that he was tired.
D) He told that he was tired.
exercises.explanation_pt: TELL + pessoa: He told ME. SAY + (that): He said (that) he was tired. NUNCA 'said me' ou 'told that' sem pessoa.

exercises.question: She is interested _____ studying medicine.
exercises.explanation_pt: Interested IN (não 'on' ou 'about'). Preposições fixas: interested in, good at, depend on, worried about, responsible for, different from.

exercises.question: Escolha:
options: A) People are very friendly in Brazil.
B) The people is very friendly in Brazil.
C) People is very friendly in Brazil.
D) Peoples are very friendly in Brazil.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'People' é PLURAL em inglês → people ARE (não 'is'). E não tem 's' — 'peoples' só existe para significar 'povos/nações'.

exercises.question: Escolha:
options: A) I don't know nothing about it.
B) I don't know anything about it.
C) I don't know none about it.
D) I know nothing not about it.
exercises.explanation_pt: Em inglês NÃO existe dupla negação. Don't + anything (não 'don't + nothing'). Ou: I know nothing (sem 'don't').

exercises.question: 'Atualmente eu moro em SP.' Em inglês:
options: A) Presently, I live in SP.
B) B e C estão corretas.
C) Actually, I live in SP.
D) Currently, I live in SP.
exercises.explanation_pt: FALSO COGNATO: 'Actually' = na verdade (não 'atualmente'). 'Currently' ou 'Presently' = atualmente. 'Actually, I don't agree' = Na verdade, eu não concordo.

exercises.question: 'Eu assisti ao jogo ontem.' Em inglês:
options: A) I assisted the game yesterday.
B) I attended the game yesterday.
C) I watched the game yesterday.
D) B e C estão corretas.
exercises.explanation_pt: FALSO COGNATO: 'Assist' = ajudar (não assistir). 'Watch' = assistir (TV, filme). 'Attend' = comparecer/assistir (aula, evento). Ambos B e C são aceitáveis.

exercises.question: I have lived here _____ five years.
exercises.explanation_pt: FOR + período de tempo (five years, two months). SINCE + ponto no tempo (2020, Monday, January). I have lived here FOR five years / SINCE 2021.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual está CORRETA? (Erro: posição do advérbio)
exercises.question: Escolha:
exercises.explanation_pt: Advérbios de frequência (always, never, usually, often) ficam DEPOIS do verbo 'to be' e ANTES de outros verbos. I AM always happy. I always GO to school.

exercises.question: The professor recommended that every student _____ the reading before class.
exercises.explanation_pt: Subjuntivo após 'recommend/suggest/insist/demand that': verbo na forma BASE (sem -s, sem -ed). That every student complete (não 'completes').

exercises.question: It is essential that the patient _____ the medication as prescribed.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'It is essential/important/necessary that' + sujeito + verbo BASE. That the patient take (não 'takes').

exercises.sentence: The committee insisted that the report _____ submitted by Friday. (be)
exercises.explanation_pt: Subjuntivo: 'insisted that the report BE submitted' (não 'is'). 'Be' é a forma base do verbo to be no subjuntivo.

exercises.question: The doctor suggested that she _____ more water daily.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Suggested that she drink' — subjuntivo. Forma base sem -s, mesmo com 'she'.

exercises.sentence: It is imperative that the government _____ action immediately. (take)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'It is imperative that' + subjuntivo. That the government take action (forma base).

exercises.question: Qual usa o subjuntivo corretamente?
options: A) I suggest that he go home.
B) I suggest that he goes home.
C) I suggest that he is going home.
D) I suggest that he went home.
exercises.explanation_pt: Subjuntivo: suggest that he GO (forma base, sem -s). Comum em escrita acadêmica e TOEFL.

exercises.sentence: The university requires that all applicants _____ proficiency in English. (demonstrate)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Requires that applicants demonstrate' — subjuntivo com forma base do verbo.

exercises.question: Qual frase tem ERRO gramatical?
options: A) It is vital that the experiment is repeated.
B) The professor recommended that we read chapter 5.
C) She insisted that he apologize.
D) The law demands that every citizen pay taxes.
exercises.explanation_pt: Erro na C: deveria ser 'that the experiment BE repeated' (subjuntivo), não 'is repeated'. As outras estão corretas com forma base.

exercises.sentence: The proposal demands that the company _____ its carbon emissions by 50%. (reduce)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Demands that the company reduce' — subjuntivo. Forma base sem -s.

exercises.question: Em quais situações usamos o subjuntivo em inglês?
options: B) Após verbos como recommend, suggest, insist, demand, require + that
exercises.explanation_pt: Subjuntivo: após recommend, suggest, insist, demand, require, propose + that. Também após 'It is essential/important/vital/necessary that'. Muito comum no TOEFL e escrita acadêmica.

exercises.question: 'Because she felt tired, she went to bed early.' Forma reduzida:
options: A) To feel tired, she went to bed early.
B) Felt tired, she went to bed early.
C) Feeling tired, she went to bed early.
D) She feeling tired went to bed early.
exercises.explanation_pt: Participle clause com -ing: 'Feeling tired, she went to bed early.' O gerúndio substitui 'Because she felt'. Sujeito deve ser o mesmo nas duas orações.

exercises.question: 'The report, which was published in 2023, revealed alarming trends.' Forma reduzida:
options: A) The report, published in 2023, revealed alarming trends.
B) The report, being published in 2023, revealed alarming trends.
C) The report, publishing in 2023, revealed alarming trends.
D) Published the report in 2023 revealed alarming trends.
exercises.explanation_pt: Past participle clause: 'published in 2023' substitui 'which was published in 2023'. Voz passiva reduzida.

exercises.sentence: _____ in a small town, she had limited access to good schools. (grow up — passado)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Having + particípio' para ação anterior à principal. Having grown up = Tendo crescido (primeiro cresceu, depois teve acesso limitado).

exercises.question: 'After he had finished the exam, he left the room.' Forma reduzida:
options: A) Finishing the exam, he left the room.
B) Having finished the exam, he left the room.
C) To finish the exam, he left the room.
D) Finished the exam, he left the room.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Having finished' = tendo terminado. Para ação completada ANTES da outra ação. Having + past participle.

exercises.question: _____ by the results, the researchers decided to repeat the experiment.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Surprised by the results' — os pesquisadores FORAM surpreendidos (passivo). Past participle para voz passiva reduzida.

exercises.sentence: _____ the data carefully, the team identified several errors. (analyze)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Analyzing the data' = Analisando os dados (ação simultânea). Present participle para ações simultâneas ou causa.

exercises.question: Qual frase tem erro de participle clause?
options: A) Feeling hungry, I ordered a pizza.
B) Walking home, the rain started. (QUEM está andando?)
C) Having studied all night, she passed the exam.
D) Built in 1900, the church is a historic landmark.
exercises.explanation_pt: Erro em A: 'Walking home, the rain started' — parece que a CHUVA está andando! O sujeito deve ser o mesmo. Correto: 'Walking home, I got caught in the rain.' Isso é chamado de 'dangling modifier'.

exercises.sentence: _____ from a distance, the mountain looks like a pyramid. (see)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Seen from a distance' = Vista de longe (a montanha É vista — passivo). Past participle para voz passiva reduzida.

exercises.question: 'Not _____ what to do, she asked her professor for advice.'
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Not knowing what to do' = Não sabendo o que fazer. Negativa de participle clause: Not + gerúndio.

exercises.question: Quais são os 3 tipos de participle clauses?
options: C) Present participle (-ing), Past participle (-ed), Perfect participle (having + pp)
exercises.explanation_pt: 3 tipos: 1) Present participle (-ing): ação simultânea/causa. 2) Past participle (-ed/irregular): passivo reduzido. 3) Perfect participle (having + pp): ação anterior. Muito usados em escrita acadêmica/TOEFL.

exercises.sentence: The study _____ light on the causes of the disease. (revelar)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Shed light on' = lançar luz sobre, revelar, esclarecer. Collocation fixa muito usada em textos acadêmicos.

exercises.sentence: The evidence _____ the conclusion that smoking causes cancer. (apoiar)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Evidence supports a conclusion' = evidência apoia uma conclusão. Collocation acadêmica padrão.

exercises.sentence: The researchers _____ an experiment to test the hypothesis. (realizar)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Conduct an experiment/study/research' = realizar um experimento. Collocation acadêmica formal.

exercises.sentence: The theory _____ into question by recent findings. (questionar)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Call into question' = questionar, colocar em dúvida. Was called into question = foi questionada.

exercises.sentence: The article _____ attention to the growing problem of inequality. (chamar atenção)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Draw attention to' = chamar atenção para. The article draws attention to = O artigo chama atenção para.

exercises.sentence: The results are _____ with previous research on the topic. (consistente)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Consistent with' = consistente com, compatível com. Os resultados são consistentes com pesquisas anteriores.

exercises.sentence: Climate change poses a serious _____ to biodiversity. (ameaça)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Pose a threat to' = representar uma ameaça para. Collocation fixa: pose + threat/risk/challenge.

exercises.sentence: The government needs to _____ measures to reduce pollution. (tomar medidas)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Take measures' = tomar medidas. Collocation fixa. Também: take action, take steps, take precautions.

exercises.sentence: The study _____ the conclusion that exercise improves mental health. (chegar a)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Come to a conclusion' = chegar a uma conclusão. Também: reach a conclusion, draw a conclusion.

exercises.sentence: The findings have far-_____ implications for public health policy. (amplo)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Far-reaching implications' = implicações de longo alcance. Collocation fixa muito usada no TOEFL.

theme: Advanced Tenses — Perfect Continuous + Future Perfect

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com o Present Perfect Continuous:
exercises.sentence: She _____ English for three years now. (study)
exercises.explanation_pt: Present Perfect Continuous: has/have been + -ing. Para ações que começaram no passado e CONTINUAM. She has been studying = Ela vem estudando (e ainda estuda).

exercises.question: 'I _____ three books this month.' (completei)
exercises.explanation_pt: Present Perfect SIMPLE para ações COMPLETADAS: I have read 3 books (li 3 livros inteiros). Continuous seria para ação em progresso: I have been reading a lot (tenho lido muito).

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com o Past Perfect Continuous:
exercises.sentence: They _____ for two hours before the bus finally arrived. (wait)
exercises.explanation_pt: Past Perfect Continuous: had been + -ing. Para ação em progresso ANTES de outra ação passada. Estavam esperando por 2h antes do ônibus chegar.

exercises.sentence: By next June, I _____ my medical degree. (complete)
exercises.explanation_pt: Future Perfect: will have + particípio. Para ação que estará COMPLETA em um momento futuro. By next June = até junho que vem, terei completado.

exercises.instruction_pt: Complete com o Future Perfect Continuous:
exercises.sentence: By the time she retires, she _____ at the hospital for 30 years. (work)
exercises.explanation_pt: Future Perfect Continuous: will have been + -ing. Para ação em progresso até um ponto futuro. Quando se aposentar, terá estado trabalhando por 30 anos.

exercises.question: 'By 2030, scientists _____ this phenomenon for over a decade.' (estudo contínuo)
exercises.explanation_pt: Future Perfect Continuous: ação em progresso até um ponto futuro. Will have been studying = terão estado estudando (ação contínua, não pontual).

exercises.sentence: He looks exhausted. He _____ all night. (study)
exercises.explanation_pt: Present Perfect Continuous para explicar resultado presente visível. Ele parece exausto porque ESTEVE estudando a noite toda (ação recente em progresso com resultado visível agora).

exercises.question: 'I have lived here for 5 years' vs 'I have been living here for 5 years'. Diferença?
options: A) Nenhuma diferença prática neste caso
C) A primeira é formal, a segunda é informal
D) A primeira é presente, a segunda é passado
exercises.explanation_pt: Com verbos como 'live', 'work', 'study' + 'for/since', ambos são aceitáveis e praticamente iguais. A forma continuous enfatiza mais a duração/continuidade, mas o significado é o mesmo.

exercises.sentence: By the end of this year, we _____ over 1,000 exercises for the app. (create)
exercises.explanation_pt: Future Perfect: ação completa até um ponto futuro. By the end of this year, we will have created = até o final do ano, teremos criado.

exercises.question: Combine: Present Perfect = ?, Past Perfect = ?, Future Perfect = ?
options: B) Ação passada com relevância presente → Ação anterior a outra passada → Ação completa antes de um ponto futuro
exercises.explanation_pt: Present Perfect: passado → relevância agora. Past Perfect: antes de outro passado. Future Perfect: completo antes de um ponto futuro. + Continuous em qualquer um = ênfase na duração/processo.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual frase está CORRETA? (Erro: subject-verb agreement com 'neither/either')
exercises.question: Escolha:
options: A) Neither the students nor the teacher have been prepared.
B) Neither the students nor the teacher were prepared.
C) Neither the students nor the teacher are prepared.
D) Neither the students nor the teacher was prepared.
exercises.explanation_pt: Com 'neither...nor' / 'either...or', o verbo concorda com o sujeito MAIS PRÓXIMO. 'The teacher' (singular) → 'was'. Se fosse 'Neither the teacher nor the students', seria 'were'.

exercises.question: Escolha a frase sem dangling modifier:
options: A) Walking to school, the rain started.
B) Walking to school, I got caught in the rain.
C) Reading the book, it fell on the floor.
D) Driving to work, the tire went flat.
exercises.explanation_pt: Dangling modifier: o sujeito da participle clause deve ser o mesmo da oração principal. A) a chuva caminhava? C) o pneu dirigia? D) o livro lia? Apenas B está correta: EU caminhava e EU fui pego pela chuva.

exercises.question: Escolha a frase com estrutura paralela correta:
options: A) She enjoys reading, swimming, and to cook.
B) She enjoys to read, swimming, and cooking.
C) She enjoys reading, to swim, and cooking.
D) She enjoys reading, swimming, and cooking.
exercises.explanation_pt: Parallel structure: itens em uma lista devem ter a mesma forma. Reading, swimming, cooking (todos gerúndio). NUNCA misturar gerúndio com infinitivo na mesma lista.

exercises.question: Escolha:
options: A) There are less students in this class than last year.
B) There are fewer students in this class than last year.
C) There is fewer students in this class.
D) There are less student in this class than last year.
exercises.explanation_pt: FEWER + contáveis plurais (students, books, cars). LESS + incontáveis (water, time, money). Fewer students, less time.

exercises.question: Escolha:
options: A) Whom gave you the book?
B) Who did you give the book to?
C) To who did you give the book?
D) To whom did you give the book?
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Whom' = objeto (quem recebe a ação). 'Who' = sujeito (quem faz a ação). 'To whom did you give...' = Para quem você deu... Formal/acadêmico.

exercises.question: Pollution _____ air quality in urban areas.
exercises.explanation_pt: AFFECT = verbo (afetar). EFFECT = substantivo (efeito). Pollution affects air quality. Pollution has an EFFECT on air quality.

exercises.question: Escolha:
options: A) It's important that the university maintains it's standards.
B) Its important that the university maintains its standards.
C) It's important that the university maintains its standards.
D) Its important that the university maintains it's standards.
exercises.explanation_pt: IT'S = it is (contração). ITS = possessivo (dele/dela para coisas). 'It is important' → It's. 'The university's standards' → its standards.

exercises.question: Escolha:
options: A) The results are more significant as previous studies
B) The results of this study are more significant than those of previous studies.
C) The results of this study are more significant than
D) The results of this study are more significant
exercises.explanation_pt: Comparações devem ser COMPLETAS: 'more significant THAN those of previous studies'. E use 'than' (não 'as' para comparativos). 'Those' evita repetir 'the results'.

exercises.question: If I had studied medicine, I _____ a doctor now.
exercises.explanation_pt: Mixed conditional: If + past perfect (passado irreal) → would + base verb (resultado presente). Se eu tivesse estudado medicina, eu SERIA médico agora (mas não sou).

exercises.question: Qual frase NÃO tem redundância?
options: A) The study revealed that exercise improves health.
B) The reason is because he was late.
C) In my opinion, I think that
D) She returned back to her hometown.
exercises.explanation_pt: A) 'returned back' = redundante (return já significa voltar). B) 'reason is because' = redundante (use 'reason is that'). C) 'In my opinion, I think' = redundante (escolha um). D) Sem redundância — frase limpa e correta.

exercises.question: I need to _____ my car repaired. (mandar consertar)
exercises.explanation_pt: Have + object + past participle = mandar alguém fazer algo. 'Have my car repaired' = mandar consertar meu carro (outra pessoa faz).

exercises.question: She _____ her hair cut yesterday. (cortou no salão)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'She had her hair cut' = Ela cortou o cabelo (no salão, outra pessoa cortou). Se ela cortasse sozinha seria 'She cut her hair'.

exercises.sentence: I'm going to _____ my eyes tested next week. (get/have)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Get + object + past participle' = mesmo significado de 'have something done'. Get my eyes tested = mandar examinar meus olhos. 'Get' é mais informal que 'have'.

exercises.question: 'I cut my hair' vs 'I had my hair cut'. Diferença?
options: A) Na primeira EU cortei, na segunda ALGUÉM cortou para mim
C) A primeira é passado, a segunda é presente
exercises.explanation_pt: 'I cut my hair' = eu mesma cortei. 'I had my hair cut' = mandei alguém cortar (salão). A estrutura causativa indica que outra pessoa fez o serviço.

exercises.sentence: The professor made the students _____ the essay again. (rewrite)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Make + person + bare infinitive' (sem 'to'). Made the students rewrite = fez os alunos reescreverem. 'Make' = forçar/obrigar.

exercises.question: She let her children _____ up late on weekends.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Let + person + bare infinitive' (sem 'to'). Let her children stay = deixou os filhos ficarem acordados. 'Let' = permitir.

exercises.sentence: I need to get my passport _____. It expires next month. (renew)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Get + object + past participle'. Get my passport renewed = mandar renovar meu passaporte.

exercises.question: Qual frase está correta?
options: A) I had my computer repairing.
B) I had repaired my computer.
C) I had my computer repaired.
D) I had my computer repair.
exercises.explanation_pt: Causativa: have + object + PAST PARTICIPLE. 'I had my computer repaired' = mandei consertar meu computador.

exercises.sentence: The teacher had us _____ in groups of four. (work)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Have + person + bare infinitive' = fazer alguém fazer algo (sem forçar como 'make'). Had us work = fez a gente trabalhar.

exercises.question: Combine: Have/Get + object + past participle = ? | Make + person + infinitive = ? | Let + person + infinitive = ?
options: A) Have/Get = serviço por terceiro | Make = obrigar | Let = permitir
B) Todas significam a mesma coisa
C) Have = passado | Make = presente | Let = futuro
exercises.explanation_pt: Have/Get something done = mandar fazer (serviço). Make someone do = obrigar. Let someone do = permitir. Três estruturas diferentes com significados diferentes.

exercises.sentence: The study was carried _____ by researchers at Harvard.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Carry out' = realizar, conduzir. The study was carried out = O estudo foi realizado.

exercises.sentence: The decision was made _____ behalf of the entire committee.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'On behalf of' = em nome de, em representação de. On behalf of the committee = em nome do comitê.

exercises.sentence: _____ addition to the tuition fee, students must pay for books and materials.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'In addition to' = além de. In addition to the tuition = além da mensalidade.

exercises.sentence: The project failed _____ account of poor planning.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'On account of' = por causa de (formal). On account of poor planning = por causa de planejamento ruim.

exercises.sentence: The new policy goes _____ effect next month.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Go into effect' = entrar em vigor. The policy goes into effect = A política entra em vigor.

exercises.sentence: The results are _____ line with our expectations.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'In line with' = de acordo com, alinhado com. In line with expectations = de acordo com as expectativas.

exercises.sentence: _____ the absence of clear evidence, the case was dismissed.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'In the absence of' = na ausência de. In the absence of evidence = na ausência de evidência.

exercises.sentence: The professor spoke _____ length about climate change.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'At length' = demoradamente, extensamente. Spoke at length = falou extensamente.

exercises.sentence: _____ contrast to the previous study, this one found no significant differences.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'In contrast to' = em contraste com. In contrast to the previous study = em contraste com o estudo anterior.

exercises.sentence: The treatment was effective _____ a large extent.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'To a large extent' = em grande medida. The treatment was effective to a large extent = eficaz em grande medida.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escolha a versão mais acadêmica e cautelosa:
exercises.question: Qual é mais apropriada para escrita acadêmica?
options: B) Exercise may help reduce symptoms of depression.
exercises.explanation_pt: Em escrita acadêmica, evitamos afirmações absolutas. 'May help reduce symptoms' é cauteloso (hedging). NUNCA use 'cures', 'definitely', 'always' sem evidência absoluta.

exercises.sentence: The results _____ that diet affects mental health. (suggest — não 'prove')
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Suggest' é mais cauteloso que 'prove'. Em escrita acadêmica: suggest, indicate, appear to, seem to > prove, show, demonstrate.

exercises.question: 'Social media causes depression in teenagers.' Versão cautelosa:
options: A) Social media always causes depression
B) Social media has no effect on depression
C) Social media is the only cause of teen depression.
D) Social media may contribute to depression in some teenagers.
exercises.explanation_pt: Hedging: 'may contribute to' (pode contribuir) + 'some teenagers' (alguns). Evita generalização absoluta.

exercises.sentence: This _____ to be the most effective treatment available. (appear)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Appears to be' = parece ser. Mais cauteloso que 'is'. Hedging modal: appears, seems, tends to.

exercises.question: Escolha:
options: A) Climate change will destroy all life on Earth
B) Climate change always causes floods
C) Climate change is not a real problem
D) Climate change could potentially have devastating consequences for biodiversity.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Could potentially have' = poderia potencialmente ter. Hedging com modal (could) + adverb (potentially). Cauteloso mas não fraco.

exercises.sentence: The data _____ indicate a correlation between sleep and academic performance. (seem)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Seem to indicate' = parecem indicar. Hedging com 'seem to' — cauteloso sobre a conclusão.

exercises.question: 'It is widely believed that early intervention tends to produce better outcomes in most cases.' Quantos hedging devices há?
exercises.explanation_pt: 3 hedging devices: 'widely believed' (não 'proven'), 'tends to' (não 'always'), 'in most cases' (não 'always/in all cases').

exercises.sentence: _____ the limitations of this study, the findings provide valuable insights. (despite)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Despite the limitations' = apesar das limitações. Reconhecer limitações é parte essencial da escrita acadêmica.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual é MUITO forte para escrita acadêmica?
exercises.question: Qual frase é inadequada para um ensaio acadêmico?
options: A) This may be attributed to...
B) It is obvious that anyone can see that...
exercises.explanation_pt: 'It is obvious that anyone can see that' é muito forte e informal. Em escrita acadêmica, evite 'obvious', 'everyone knows', 'clearly'. Use hedging.

exercises.question: Por que hedging é importante na escrita acadêmica?
options: B) Para evitar afirmações absolutas que podem ser refutadas, mostrando rigor intelectual
D) Para tornar o texto mais longo
exercises.explanation_pt: Hedging mostra RIGOR INTELECTUAL — o autor reconhece que a ciência raramente é absoluta. Afirmações absolutas são facilmente refutadas. Hedging não é fraqueza — é precisão.

theme: Complex Sentence Structures for TOEFL Writing

exercises.instruction_pt: Combine as frases usando uma cláusula relativa:
exercises.question: 'The study was published in 2023. It examined the effects of social media on teenagers.' Combinada:
options: A) The study was published in 2023, which examined the effects of social media on teenagers.
B) The study, which was published in 2023, examined the effects of social media on teenagers.
C) Published in 2023, the study which examined social media on teenagers.
D) The study examined, which was published in 2023, the effects of social media.
exercises.explanation_pt: Cláusula relativa não-restritiva (entre vírgulas): 'The study, which was published in 2023, examined...' Informação adicional que pode ser removida sem mudar o significado essencial.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escolha a frase com a melhor estrutura para TOEFL Writing:
exercises.question: Qual é mais sofisticada gramaticalmente?
options: A) Not only does exercise reduce stress, but it also improves sleep quality, making it one of the most effective strategies for maintaining overall health.
B) Exercise reduces stress and sleep is improved by exercise too
C) Exercise is good. It reduces stress. It improves sleep
D) Exercise is good because it reduces stress and improves sleep
exercises.explanation_pt: C usa: inversão ('Not only does'), correlative conjunction ('not only...but also'), participial clause ('making it'), e vocabulário acadêmico. Essa complexidade gramatical impressiona no TOEFL Writing.

exercises.sentence: It _____ not until the 20th century _____ women gained the right to vote in most countries.
exercises.explanation_pt: Cleft sentence enfática: 'It was not until X that Y happened.' Enfatiza o tempo: não foi até o século 20 QUE mulheres ganharam o direito de voto.

exercises.instruction_pt: Escolha a frase com estrutura paralela correta:
exercises.question: Qual tem paralelismo perfeito?
options: A) The program aims at education, inspire, and creating leaders.
B) The program aims to educate, to inspire, and to create leaders.
C) The program aims to educate, inspire, and the creation of leaders.
D) The program aims to educate, inspiring, and create leaders.
exercises.explanation_pt: Paralelismo: todos os itens na mesma forma. 'To educate, to inspire, and to create' — todos infinitivos. Ou: 'educate, inspire, and create' (sem 'to' repetido).

exercises.sentence: _____ effective the new policy may be, it cannot solve the problem entirely on its own.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'However + adjective + subject + may be' = Por mais [adj] que seja. However effective it may be = Por mais eficaz que a política possa ser.

exercises.question: 'The lack of funding, not the quality of research, caused the project to fail.' Com cleft sentence:
options: A) The project failed because of funding
B) What caused the project to fail was the lack of funding, not the quality of research.
C) The project was caused to fail by research quality
D) It was the quality of research that caused failure
exercises.explanation_pt: 'What caused X was Y' = O que causou X foi Y. Cleft sentence com 'What' enfatiza a causa real (falta de financiamento, não qualidade da pesquisa).

exercises.sentence: _____ important as grades are, they do not fully reflect a student's potential.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'As + adjective + as + subject + verb' = Por mais [adj] que. As important as grades are = Por mais importantes que as notas sejam.

exercises.question: 'The first study found X. The second study found Y.' Como conectar?
options: A) And the second study found Y
B) While the first study found X, the second study, conducted under different conditions, yielded contrasting results.
C) But the second study found Y
D) The second study also found stuff
exercises.explanation_pt: C é sofisticada: usa 'While' (contraste temporal), participial clause ('conducted under different conditions'), e vocabulário acadêmico ('yielded contrasting results').

exercises.sentence: Only through sustained effort and dedication _____ students achieve fluency in a foreign language.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Only through X can Y happen' — inversão com 'only'. Only through sustained effort can students achieve = Somente através de esforço sustentado podem estudantes alcançar.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual frase receberia a MAIOR nota no TOEFL Writing?
exercises.question: Escolha:
options: A) Pollution is bad for health
B) Pollution is a problem that affects many people and causes diseases
C) Pollution kills people and makes them sick everywhere in the world

D) The detrimental effects of environmental pollution on public health have been extensively documented, with studies consistently linking prolonged exposure to airborne particulates to elevated rates of respiratory disease, cardiovascular complications, and premature mortality.
exercises.explanation_pt: C usa: vocabulário acadêmico (detrimental, extensively, prolonged, elevated, mortality), estrutura complexa (participial clause, prepositional phrases), hedging implícito ('have been documented', 'linking...to'). Isso é nível C1 — o que o TOEFL Writing premia.

theme: Revisão integrada — Erros avançados do TOEFL

exercises.question: 'The data shows that the hypothesis was correct.' O erro é:
options: C) 'shows' — data é plural, deveria ser 'show'
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Data' é PLURAL em inglês acadêmico (singular = 'datum'). The data SHOW (não 'shows'). No TOEFL, usar 'data' como plural é preferido.

exercises.question: 'Each of the students have submitted their assignment.' O erro:
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Each' é SINGULAR → each HAS (não 'have'). 'Each of the students has submitted.' Nota: 'their' como pronome singular neutro é aceito no inglês moderno.

exercises.question: 'The amount of students enrolled in the program has increased.' O erro:
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Amount' = para incontáveis (amount of water). 'Number' = para contáveis (number of students). The NUMBER of students.

exercises.question: 'Despite of the rain, the event was successful.' O erro:
exercises.explanation_pt: DESPITE (sem 'of'). Ou: IN SPITE OF. NUNCA 'despite of'. Despite the rain = Apesar da chuva.

exercises.question: 'The professor, along with her students, are conducting research.' O erro:
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Along with', 'together with', 'as well as' NÃO mudam o número do sujeito. O sujeito é 'The professor' (singular) → IS. Diferente de 'and' que tornaria plural.

exercises.question: 'He is one of the few scientists who has published in that journal.' O erro:
options: C) 'one of the few' — deveria ser 'one of the many'
D) 'has' — deveria ser 'have' (who refere-se a 'scientists', plural)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Who' refere-se a 'scientists' (plural), não a 'one'. 'Scientists who HAVE published' (não 'has'). Este é um dos erros mais comuns no TOEFL.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual frase tem erro de pronome?
exercises.question: Identifique:
options: A) The committee made its decision yesterday.
B) A student should complete your homework on time.
C) A student should complete their homework on time.
D) Everyone should bring their own laptop.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Your' é segunda pessoa — não combina com 'a student' (terceira pessoa). Deveria ser 'their' (pronome neutro singular aceito) ou 'his or her'. As outras estão corretas.

exercises.question: 'The new policy is designed to reduce costs, improving efficiency, and to enhance quality.' O erro:
options: D) 'improving' — deveria ser 'improve' (para manter paralelismo com 'reduce' e 'enhance')
exercises.explanation_pt: Paralelismo quebrado: 'to reduce, improving, to enhance'. Deveria ser: 'to reduce, improve, and enhance' (todos infinitivos sem 'to') ou 'to reduce, to improve, and to enhance'.

exercises.question: 'The reason why the experiment failed is because the temperature was too high.' O erro:
options: D) 'is because' — deveria ser 'is that' (redundância: reason + because)
exercises.explanation_pt: 'The reason is because' é REDUNDANTE (reason já implica causa). Correto: 'The reason is THAT the temperature was too high.' Ou: 'The experiment failed BECAUSE the temperature was too high.'

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual frase é perfeita para o TOEFL?
exercises.question: Escolha a frase sem NENHUM erro:
options: A) Data shows a correlation of socioeconomic status and educational attainment, a finding consistent to previous research
B) The datas show that socioeconomic status effects educational attainment, which is consistent to previous research
C) The data suggest that there is a significant correlation among socioeconomic status and educational attainment
D) The data suggest that there is a significant correlation between socioeconomic status and educational attainment, a finding that is consistent with previous research in this field.
exercises.explanation_pt: A é perfeita: 'data suggest' (plural), 'correlation between X and Y' (não 'of' ou 'among' para dois itens), 'consistent with' (não 'to'), 'attainment' (vocabulário acadêmico), cláusula apositiva correta. B: 'datas' não existe, 'effects' deveria ser 'affects', 'consistent to' errado. C: 'shows' singular. D: 'among' é para 3+ itens, deveria ser 'between'.

exercises.question: If I had studied medicine, I _____ a doctor now.
exercises.explanation_pt: Mixed Conditional tipo 1: If + past perfect (passado irreal) → would + base verb (resultado presente). Se eu tivesse estudado medicina, eu SERIA médico agora.

exercises.question: If she were more careful, she _____ the mistake yesterday.
exercises.explanation_pt: Mixed Conditional tipo 2: If + past simple (situação geral presente) → would have + particípio (resultado passado). Se ela FOSSE mais cuidadosa (em geral), não TERIA COMETIDO o erro ontem.

exercises.sentence: If he hadn't missed the flight, he _____ in London right now. (be)
exercises.explanation_pt: Passado irreal → resultado presente: 'would be'. Se ele não tivesse perdido o voo, estaria em Londres AGORA.

exercises.question: 'If I spoke Chinese, I would have gotten the job in Shanghai last year.' Isso é:
exercises.explanation_pt: Mixed: If I spoke (presente geral — não falo chinês) → I would have gotten (resultado passado — não consegui o emprego). Situação presente afetando resultado passado.

exercises.sentence: If they had invested in education 20 years ago, the country _____ more developed today. (be)
exercises.explanation_pt: If + past perfect (passado) → would + base (presente). Ação passada com consequência no presente.

exercises.question: Qual Mixed Conditional está correto?
options: A) If I would have known, I would tell you
B) If I had known, I would tell you now
C) If I knew, I would have told you
D) B e C estão corretos (tipos diferentes de mixed conditional).
exercises.explanation_pt: B = passado→presente (se eu tivesse sabido, te diria agora). C = presente→passado (se eu soubesse em geral, teria te dito). Ambos são mixed conditionals válidos.

exercises.sentence: If she weren't so shy, she _____ the speech at yesterday's ceremony. (give)
exercises.explanation_pt: Present → Past mixed: If she weren't (presente geral) → would have given (passado). Se ela não fosse tímida (em geral), teria feito o discurso ontem.

exercises.question: If we had left earlier, we _____ stuck in traffic right now.
exercises.explanation_pt: Past → Present: Se tivéssemos saído mais cedo, NÃO ESTARÍAMOS presos no trânsito AGORA. 'Wouldn't be' = resultado presente.

exercises.sentence: If I were a native English speaker, I _____ need to study for the TOEFL. (not)
exercises.explanation_pt: Second Conditional simples (mas relevante para mixed): If I were (presente irreal) → wouldn't need (resultado presente). Se eu fosse nativo, não precisaria do TOEFL.

exercises.question: Quais são os dois tipos de Mixed Conditional?
options: C) Passado→Presente (had done → would do) e Presente→Passado (did → would have done)
D) São iguais ao Third Conditional
exercises.explanation_pt: Tipo 1: If + past perfect → would + base (ação passada, resultado presente). Tipo 2: If + past simple → would have + particípio (situação presente, resultado passado).

theme: Ellipsis e Substitution (redução em escrita acadêmica)
